mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% in low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment rise.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail and housing fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a confirmed economic expansion reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative 20-day equity returns in > 50% of expansion-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings crossing above 2.5 percent year-over-year trigger expectations of imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above 2.5% are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly payroll employment growth sustains household income streams and aggregate consumption demand, providing a stable earnings floor that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth series shows zero predictive power for 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decoupling where quarterly GDP growth decelerates while monthly retail sales accelerate signals unsustainable consumption borrowing, leading to a demand correction and negative S&P 500 returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production confirm a supply-side contraction, reducing corporate output and profit expectations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below previous month value for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Consecutive industrial production declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 55% of historical instances.
